history.title: History
history.pickHint: Click measurements in the list to add them to the chart.
history.clickHint: Click to add / remove a measurement
history.notConfiguredBody: History reads stored measurements back from InfluxDB. Enable it in Config → InfluxDB to use this view.
history.footer: Times in local time · data read back from InfluxDB · hover for values · shaded band (min/max) shown for a single measurement.
energy.title: Energy
energy.notConfigured: InfluxDB is not configured — enable it in Config → InfluxDB to use Energy.
energy.noDaily: No daily data in this range.
energy.footer: Daily import vs export (kWh) · totals are the month's counter delta · local time · read from InfluxDB.
energy.fieldsIntro: Pick which cumulative counters to total each month for this device — auto-detected from its energy registers (Wh / varh / VAh).
energy.noFields: No cumulative energy counters (Wh / varh / VAh) in this device’s selected measurements. Add some in Measurements first.
registers.title: All measurements
config.title: Configuration
vmeters.title: Virtual Meters
firstrun.title: Connect your first device
firstrun.body: No data yet. Open your device in Devices, set its address (IP / port) in the Edit tab, then Save & Apply.
valhist.title: History
valhist.needInflux: Enable InfluxDB (Config → InfluxDB) to see history for this value.
valhist.noData: No history stored yet for this value.
valhist.hint: Click any live value on the dashboard to see its recent history.
devices.title: Devices
devices.rtuSoon: Modbus RTU support is coming soon
devices.wizard.mqttTopicHint: The device subscribes here; values are read from the JSON payload by the template’s json_path. + and # wildcards supported.
devices.editPassKeep: Leave the password blank to keep the stored one.
devices.deleteConfirm:
Delete this device?

Yes: stops its polling and removes it from config.yaml (its measurement selection file is kept on disk).
No: keeps everything as is.
devices.wizard.protoLocked: Fixed after creation — the template map is transport-specific.
devices.wizard.noTplForProto: No {proto} templates yet — upload or create one for this protocol.
devices.wizard.rtuNote: The serial adapter must be attached to the host and mapped into the container (e.g. devices: /dev/ttyUSB0:/dev/ttyUSB0). Polling starts right after saving.
devices.wizard.tplIntro: The template is the measurement map of the equipment. Pick one from the library — creating and uploading templates arrives in the next phase.
devices.wizard.name: Device name
devtpl.title: Device Template
devtpl.overwriteConfirm:
A template with this id already exists.

Yes: overwrite it with the uploaded file.
No: cancel the upload.
devtpl.deleteConfirm:
Delete this template?

Yes: removes the template file (devices are untouched).
No: keeps it.
devtpl.name: Name
devices.wizard.tplIntro2: The template is the measurement map of the equipment: pick one, upload a file, or create your own.
monitor.hintTouch: Tap a value in the list above to add it to the live graph. Tap again in the list to highlight it.
monitor.hintMouse: Click or drag measurements from the left sidebar onto the graph to monitor them in real-time. Use mouse wheel to zoom and drag to pan when zoomed.
monitor.addHint: Tap or drag values to graph
backup.title: Backup & Restore
backup.intro: Download a ZIP with all devices, measurement selections, device templates and virtual meters — or restore one. Secrets and network settings are excluded by default.
backup.importConfirm:
Restore this backup?

Yes: overwrites the current devices, measurement selections, templates and virtual meters, then reloads.
No: cancel.
mqtt.tls.title: TLS / Encryption
mqtt.tls.hint: Certificate paths are inside the container (put files under config/). Leave client cert/key empty for server-only TLS.
login.title: Sign in
login.lockedOut: Too many attempts. Try again in
security.title: Security
security.intro: Optional, off by default (this appliance is meant for a trusted LAN). Turn these on when it must be reachable from a wider network.
security.allowlist.title: IP allowlist
security.allowlist.hint: Only these IPs/subnets may reach the UI/API. One per line (e.g. 192.168.1.0/24). Empty = open. Loopback is always allowed.
security.https.title: HTTPS (web UI)
security.https.enable: Serve the UI over HTTPS (restart required)
security.https.hint: Leave paths empty to auto-generate a self-signed certificate on next start. Restart the container to apply.
security.auth.title: Login
security.auth.enable: Require login to access the UI/API
security.auth.hint: Passwords are stored hashed. Leave a password blank to keep the current one. Applies immediately.
security.restartHint: Restart the container to apply HTTPS.
mqtt.defaultTopic: Default topic prefix for new devices
mqtt.defaultTopicHint: {device} = the device id. Each device's own prefix is set in the device.
influx.defaultBucketHint: {device} = the device id. Each device's own bucket is set in the device.
devices.wizard.routeLocked: Fixed for device #1 (protects existing history & Home Assistant entities).
devices.wizard.routeLockedEdit: Fixed after creation — changing it would orphan existing history & HA entities.
devices.sink.routeLockNote: Topic prefix is fixed after creation — changing it would orphan existing history & HA entities.
devices.sink.influxLockNoteEdit: Bucket and tag are fixed after creation — changing them would orphan existing history.
devices.wizard.haDiscovery: Publish Home Assistant MQTT discovery for this device
registers.custom.title: Custom measurement
registers.custom.name: Name
registers.custom.badAddrMsg: Use a decimal or 0x… value in 0–65535.
registers.upload.primaryMsg: The primary device keeps its built-in measurement map. Upload applies to other devices.
devices.sink.mqttLockNote: Topic and MQTT output are fixed for device #1 (protects existing history & Home Assistant).
devices.sink.influxLockNote: Bucket and tag are fixed for device #1 (protects existing history).
devices.sink.httpNote: Serves this device's live values as read-only JSON, keyed by measurement name (Solar-API style) — pull it from any HTTP client without MQTT or InfluxDB. Guarded by the same IP allowlist / auth as the UI.
rest.title: REST push
rest.note: POSTs the live values of this device as JSON to the URL every interval. External URLs are allowed. Put a Bearer / API key in Headers (stored masked).
calc.title: Calculated measurements
calc.help: Derive new measurements from existing ones with a formula (e.g. power factor = P / S). Calculated values flow to MQTT, InfluxDB and the JSON output like any real measurement.
calc.empty: No calculated measurements yet. Add one, or start from a preset.
calc.name: Name
calc.label: Label
calc.bindInputs: Bind preset inputs to your fields
calc.noFields: No measurements selected on this device yet.
calc.saveAsPresetHint: Save this formula as a reusable preset
calc.needNameExpr: Name and expression are required to save a template
calc.onlyUserDeletable: Only your own saved presets can be deleted
calc.alsoIn: These also appear in Monitor, MQTT, InfluxDB and the JSON output — like any real measurement.
calc.statefulPreview: stateful — value appears once running (needs two polls)
vmeter.delete.title: Delete virtual meter
devices.wizard.httpHint: The device polls this URL and reads values by the template’s json_path. Values arrive already scaled.
devices.overview.noValues: No values yet — the device may not be polling.
registers.custom.topicHint: The topic this value is published on (+ / # wildcards). Leave json_path empty if the payload is the bare number.
registers.custom.noTopicMsg: Enter the MQTT topic this value is published on.
registers.custom.jsonPathHint: Where to read this value from the JSON response.
mqtt.ha.perDeviceHint: Per-device discovery is enabled on each device (Outputs tab).
registers.custom.noJsonPathMsg: Enter where to read this value, e.g. Body.Data.PowerReal_P_Sum.
devices.discoverModbusHint: Scans a private LAN range on the Modbus port for devices that answer. Read-only.
devices.discoverNoCidr: Enter a network range (CIDR) to scan.
devices.discoverNoHost: Enter a host / IP to scan.
devices.discoverFroniusHint: The Fronius DataManager's IP — its Solar API is queried to list the meters & inverters behind it.
status.title: System Status
status.resPending: Resource metrics require the updated backend (deploy pending).
csv.title: Import register map from CSV
csv.intro: A CSV register list from a vendor or the community becomes a device template. Columns are matched loosely — address and name are required; label, unit, type, scale, category, json_path are optional. Address accepts decimal or 0x hex.
csv.name: Name
csv.noData: Paste a CSV or open a file first.
templates.title: Templates
templates.subtitle: The catalog behind your devices. Device maps tell the gateway how to read a real meter (register addresses, function code, byte order). Meter emulations re-serve live values as a standard Modbus meter. Bundle, upload, import from CSV, edit and share them here.
templates.emuHint: Map live values into the layout a consumer expects. Run and monitor instances on the Virtual Meters page.
templates.name: Name
templates.label: Description
app.title: Multi-Bus Gateway
status.devicesTip: Devices online / total — click to open
toast.fixErrors: Please fix the errors before saving.
toast.alreadyOnGraph: This measurement is already on the graph
toast.max6: You can monitor up to 6 values at a time
toast.alreadyMonitoredMsg: This measurement is already being monitored
toast.influxTagsJson: InfluxDB tags must be valid JSON
lbl.name: Name
lbl.label: Label
msg.noRangeMap: No measurements map into this range.
msg.noMonitoredAdd: No measurements monitored. Add measurements first.
thr.minMaxHint: Leave Min/Max empty to auto-derive from thresholds.
vmeter.whichDevice: Which device's live values this meter re-serves.
vmeter.whoseDevice: Whose live values this meter re-serves.
tip.importCsv: Import a register map from a CSV (vendor/community list) and assign it to this device
tip.upload: Upload a measurement map (device template) and assign it to this device
tip.downloadMap: Download this device's full measurement map (JSON)
tip.scan: Scan the network for devices you can add
vmeter.overwriteConfirm: A template with this id already exists. Overwrite it?
write.title: Write to device
write.warn: This writes to real hardware and cannot be undone. Double-check the address and value.
write.primaryMsg: The primary device is read-only. Writes apply to other devices.
tip.write: Write a value to this device (Modbus FC5/FC6/FC16)
write.leaseHint: 0 = permanent. Else reverts to the safe value if not re-sent in time.
alerts.title: Alerts
alerts.intro: Infrastructure-health alerts (a device or sink drops, latency spikes, the InfluxDB buffer backs up) delivered over MQTT and/or an HTTP webhook. Not value alerts. Applied live on save.
alerts.webhookUrlHint: Empty = webhook off. POSTs the alert JSON here (no LAN restriction — meant for external notifiers).
alerts.badHeaders: Webhook headers must be valid JSON
alerts.badBody: Webhook body must be valid JSON
security.writes.title: Modbus writes
security.writes.hint: Off by default — writing to real hardware is irreversible. When on, only registers declared writable in a device template can be written, within their bounds, and every write is confirmed, authenticated and audited. The primary device stays read-only.
security.nonlanHttp: Allow HTTP/JSON devices with a non-LAN URL (opens an SSRF path)
general.title: General
general.timezoneHint: IANA name (e.g. Europe/Bucharest). Sets the month boundaries for the Energy report — applies immediately, no restart.
vmeter.onStaleHint: For multi-source (device.register) maps choose fail or sentinel — absence is never served as 0.
vmeter.rowStaleHint: Per-row freshness bound (s) for composite policies — empty = auto (source device / instance)
vmeter.unknownDevWarn: Warning: source prefix is not a known device id
vmeter.unknownDevWarn2: Those rows will read as plain register names (or show as missing). Save anyway?
monitor.emptyHint: Click a value in the list on the left to start charting it live.
dash.colorHint: Tip: avoid green/yellow/red — they read as status colors next to thresholds.
thr.unitHint: Thresholds are compared against the RAW value in
calc.emptySub: Derive new values with a formula — start from a preset:
calc.emptyOr: …or use + Add above for a blank formula.
vmeter.scaleHint: raw = value × scale; the consumer reads raw ÷ scale
general.defaultColorsHint: Applied to NEW widgets only — existing choices are kept. "Reapply defaults" lives in the dashboard's Customize dialog.
dash.reapplyHint: Overwrite every dashboard widget's color with the convention defaults (phase/category) from Settings → General
dash.reapplyConfirm: Overwrite ALL dashboard widget colors with the defaults from Settings → General?
dash.deviceDownDesc: The device is unreachable — check Status for details.
dash.noDeviceWidgets: No measurements on this dashboard yet
dash.noDeviceWidgetsDesc: Pick measurements for this device to see them here.
diag.title: Bus Monitor
diag.subtitle: Frame-level Modbus trace — the exact bytes on the wire, one row per transaction (every retry included). Runtime only: the buffer lives in memory and capture stops on restart.
diag.pauseTip: Pause the live view (capture keeps running)
diag.empty: No transactions captured. Start the capture and the polled traffic appears here — each retry as its own frame.
probe.title: Register Probe
probe.subtitle: One-shot read on any Modbus device with every interpretation side by side — data type × word order (ABCD / CDAB / BADC / DCBA). Read-only. With the capture running, the probe's frames show up in the monitor above, exception replies included.
probe.reading: Reading… (a failed read retries a few times before giving up)
probe.noResponse: No response — check the bus monitor above for the frame (exception code, timeout).
probe.hint: The column that yields a plausible value is the device's word order — set it as byte_order in the device template.
ss.title: SunSpec Scan
ss.subtitle: Walks the SunSpec model chain (the SunS marker at 40000/50000/0, then every model the device declares) — identity, meters, MPPT, storage. Read-only; works on most Fronius / SMA / SolarEdge / Huawei / ABB inverters and SunSpec meters.
ss.needHost: Enter the device IP or hostname.
ss.hint: Model addresses are live from the device — use them when building a template (the register block of a model starts at its address + 2).
devices.wizard.browseListening: Listening 3s — retained topics appear instantly, live ones as they publish…
devices.wizard.browsePick: Click a topic to use it as the subscribe topic.
devices.wizard.browsePicked: Topic set. Wildcards + and # still work if you generalize it.
devices.wizard.browseTruncated: Topic list truncated — narrow it with the filter field.
devices.wizard.browseEmpty: No topics heard — the broker may be idle (nothing retained, nobody publishing). Try again while the device is sending.
registers.custom.pickHint: Click a field to use its path.
registers.custom.pickEmpty: No usable fields in the payload.
registers.custom.pickNotJson: Payload is not JSON — the value is the whole message, leave the path empty.
snap.title: Snapshots & Rollback
snap.intro: A snapshot of the full config is taken automatically after every change (devices, measurements, templates, virtual meters, settings). Restore rolls everything back to that moment — the current state is snapshotted first, so a rollback is itself reversible. “Last known good” updates only after a healthy boot and restores automatically if the config no longer loads.
snap.empty: No snapshots yet — one is taken automatically after the first config change.
snap.restoreConfirm:
Roll the configuration back to this snapshot?

Devices, measurements, templates, virtual meters and settings all revert to that moment. The current state is snapshotted first, so you can roll forward again.
audit.title: Audit Trail
audit.intro: Every configuration change, login, device write and secret export — who, from which IP, when, with the payload redacted. With auth on, only the admin can read this.
audit.adminOnly: The audit trail requires the admin role.
audit.empty: Nothing recorded yet — config changes, logins and device writes will appear here.
security.auth.operator: Operator username (live actions, no config — optional)
security.auth.operatorHint: The operator can use the commissioning tools (trace, probe, discovery) and write to devices within template bounds, but cannot change any configuration.
passkeys.title: Passkeys
passkeys.hint: Password-less sign-in bound to this device (fingerprint, PIN, security key). Browsers require a secure context: register and sign in via localhost or an HTTPS hostname — a plain-HTTP IP address will not work. You can enroll now, before enabling login.
passkeys.empty: No passkeys yet. Register one from a secure context (localhost or an HTTPS hostname).
passkeys.deleteConfirm: Delete this passkey? Sign-in with it stops working immediately.
passkeys.insecure: Passkeys need a secure context — open the UI via localhost or an HTTPS hostname (not a plain-HTTP IP).
